description: Track token usage across 25 AI coding tools — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Kiro, OpenCode, Antigravity, Copilot, Kimi, CodeBuddy, WorkBuddy, Grok, Kilo, Roo, Zed, Goose, Mimo, ZCode & more — local-first, zero-config, with a dashboard, macOS menu bar app, and desktop widgets.
More is Different. A multi-agent world engine where AI agents live, talk, compete, ally.
LLM-compiled knowledge bases for any AI agent. Parallel multi-agent research, thesis-driven investigation, source ingestion, wiki compilation, querying, and artifact generation.
Token efficient Claude Code full Python rebuild. AI Coding Agent in 260K LoC pure Python. Up to 200X Cost Saving!

wanman is an open-source agent matrix runtime inspired by Japanese one-man trains. It lets human users step back into an observer role while local agent runtimes coordinate autonomous multi-agent workflows, task execution, and artifacts.

The local-first LLM Wiki: open-source knowledge graph builder, RAG knowledge base, and agent memory store. Built on Andrej Karpathy's pattern. An Obsidian alternative for personal knowledge management, AI second brain, and durable Claude Code / Codex / OpenClaw memory.
MathCode: A Frontier Mathematical Coding Agent
21 writing rules for AI coding and writing agents. Drop-in for Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, Cursor, and Aider, so their output reads like a tech pro.

Not an Amazon-style catalog or marketplace. ctx is a recommendation layer: bring your org tools or use the shipped graph to load the right skills, agents, MCPs, and harnesses only for the current dev window, cutting token bills and local compute waste: 79,958-node LLM-wiki graph, 68,494 skills, 467 agents, 10,790 MCPs, 207 harnesses.
